name: Beefy Cheesy Burritos (Upgraded)
ingredients:
FOR THE BEEF FILLING:
454g (16 oz) 93% lean ground beef
1 Tbsp avocado or olive oil
½ yellow onion, finely diced
2 garlic cloves, minced
1 packet taco seasoning (~1 oz)
1 can (14.5 oz) fire-roasted diced tomatoes, drained
¼ cup water

FOR THE CHEESY TACO MIX:
200g 2% plain Greek yogurt
100g red enchilada sauce
100g shredded Monterey Jack
30g (2 Tbsp) chopped cilantro

FOR ASSEMBLY:
6 burrito-size flour tortillas
Store-bought pickled red onions, for topping
directions:
1. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook 3–4 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
2. Add the ground beef. Break it up with a spatula and brown for 5–7 minutes, until no pink remains. Drain excess fat if needed.
3. Sprinkle the taco seasoning over the beef. Add the drained fire-roasted tomatoes and ¼ cup water. Stir and simmer 3–5 minutes until the liquid has reduced and the mixture is thick (not soupy). Remove from heat and let cool 5–10 minutes.
4. While the beef cools, make the cheesy taco mix: in a medium bowl, stir together the Greek yogurt, enchilada sauce, shredded Monterey Jack, and cilantro until smooth.
5. Warm the tortillas briefly (stack wrapped in a damp paper towel, microwave 20–30 seconds) to make them pliable.
6. Assemble each burrito: spread ~3 Tbsp of the cheesy mix down the center of a tortilla. Top with ~⅙ of the beef (about ⅓ cup). Add a small pile of pickled red onions.
7. Fold the sides in, then roll tightly from the bottom up to form a sealed burrito.
8. To serve now: pan-fry seam-side down over medium heat 2–3 minutes per side until golden.
9. To freeze: wrap each burrito individually in parchment or foil, then place in a freezer bag. Freezes well for up to 3 months.
10. To reheat from frozen: microwave 2–3 minutes. For a crisp exterior, microwave 1–1.5 minutes to defrost, then air-fry at 375°F for 6–8 minutes or pan-fry 2–3 minutes per side.
notes:
Cheese swap note: replacing the original Laughing Cow + 2% cheddar with full-fat Monterey Jack bumps fat and calories meaningfully — expect ~30–40 more cal per burrito vs. the lean original. Worth it for melt and flavor.

TIPS:
• Drain the tomatoes well — a wet filling = soggy tortilla in the freezer. If needed, simmer longer in step 3 to reduce.
• For extra crispness when freezing, brush the outside of each burrito lightly with oil before wrapping — they air-fry up beautifully.
• Pickled red onion brands that work well: Trader Joe's, Gordo's, or any jarred variety from the condiment aisle.
nutritional_info: Approx. 420–435 cal | 28g protein | 37g carbs | 18g fat per burrito (with upgrades and Monterey Jack)
